content: Multi-tenant Next.js App Router apps commonly split into a public tenant-facing tree (e.g. app/loja/[slug]/...) and an authenticated admin tree, resolving tenant context in middleware or a server layout, then enforcing that every DB query is scoped to the tenant via a query helper rather than relying on routing alone. Official Next.js multi-tenant guide (nextjs.org/docs/app/guides/multi-tenant) confirms path- or subdomain-based tenant resolution via middleware rewrites.
